projectDescription: A Java framework for building reactive, component-based web UIs with server-side rendering and real-time WebSocket-powered state synchronization.
keyFeatures: Reactive server-side components defined entirely in Java with no JavaScript required
WebSocket-based real-time state synchronization between server and browser
Component-scoped CSS and co-located JS resources via annotation processing (APT)
Built-in client-side router with @Route, @Layout, @Param and @UrlParam annotations
Bean Validation integration via @Call methods with @Valid parameters
Smart reactive collections (SmartList, SmartMap, SmartSet) for fine-grained delta updates
Spring Boot auto-configuration starter and lightweight standalone Undertow server
Redis-backed distributed state store for horizontal scaling
Handlebars-like template syntax with {{binding}}, {{#if}}, {{#each}} and slot support
publicApiSurface: HtmlComponent — base class for all components; override template(), onInit(), onMount(), onUnmount()
@Bind — exposes a field as a reactive binding to the template
@State — marks a field as server-managed reactive state
@Call — exposes a method as a callable action from the browser
@Route(path) — maps a component class to a URL path
@Layout(Class) — wraps a page component inside a layout component
@Stateful — serializes component state to Redis/persistent store
@StatefulRam — keeps component state in JVM memory only
@Stateless — encodes state cryptographically in the HTML (zero server memory)
@Client — enables client-side rendering (CSR) mode for a component
@DomMode — sets the DOM update strategy (CLIENT_MANAGED or SERVER_OWNED)
@Param / @UrlParam — injects path and query parameters into component fields
ReactiveVar<T> — observable value container with onChange listeners
EventBus — in-process pub/sub for cross-component communication
StateStore — SPI interface for pluggable state persistence (Caffeine, Redis)
JReactiveAutoConfiguration — Spring Boot auto-configuration entry point
JButton, JInput, JForm, JSelect, JCheckBox, JTable, JCard, JModal, Link — built-in UI component library
